modules.text:
FinRecon is a financial data reconciliation engine built at Bank of America for EMBS (Enterprise Mortgage-Backed Securities).

It automates reconciliation across 4 distributed database systems (Sybase IQ, Sybase ASE, Oracle, Netezza) with anomaly detection and comprehensive runbook documentation.

modules.title: The Challenge
modules.text:
Bank of America processes millions of mortgage-backed securities records monthly across Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae, and Ginnie Mae agency pools.

Manual reconciliation was time-consuming, error-prone, and created bottlenecks in critical production cycles. The system needed to handle 100,000+ record datasets with sub-second query performance.

modules.title: Solution & Impact
modules.text:
Built an automated reconciliation engine with intelligent anomaly detection across 4 database systems.

Implemented sophisticated SQL query optimization through indexing and batch chunking strategies.

Reduced manual overhead by ~60% and improved query performance by ~45%.

modules.title: Key Features & Reliability
modules.text:
Multi-source data reconciliation with anomaly detection and comprehensive runbook-style documentation for incident response.

Batch processing for 100,000+ record datasets with intelligent indexing strategies and detailed audit trails.

Achieved zero SLA breaches across an 18-month tenure.

modules.title: Production Excellence
modules.text:
Implemented comprehensive monitoring via Splunk and AppDynamics for real-time visibility into batch cycles.

Developed detailed runbook documentation for 12+ incident types, enabling rapid incident response and resolution.

Contributed Java Spring enhancements to the broader EMBS platform infrastructure.
